page: Amazon Web Services c8in.8xlarge is an x86_64 architecture compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel processors. It features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on external network-attached block storage. Network capabilities are a key feature of this instance, providing a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, which supports high-throughput data transfers and EBS optimization. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8in.8xlarge offers a high ratio of compute power and network bandwidth relative to its memory allocation. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and the complete absence of local storage, which makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space. It is designed for compute-bound workloads, network-intensive applications, batch processing, and distributed analytics that benefit from high baseline network performance and dedicated Intel CPU resources.
description: Amazon Web Services c8in.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical Intel cores operating at 3.9 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration results in a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, requiring reliance on network-attached storage. However, it provides a high baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for network-heavy and compute-bound tasks, though the lack of local storage and moderate memory capacity present clear tradeoffs for data-intensive applications. It is best suited for batch processing, network-intensive applications, and distributed compute workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel processors, high-speed network bandwidth, and a low memory-to-core ratio for compute-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz clock speed
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput transfers
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with no local storage
Optimized for compute-bound and network-intensive workloads
